title: Crocodile Watches the Pieces Move
description: On pages 5–9, this part focuses on crocodile watches the pieces move, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: Crocodile Watches the Pieces Move. Crocodile uses Rain Dinners and Baroque Works to capture his enemies and reveal the shape of Operation Utopia. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Hey, look over there!

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Welcome, sirs!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Please come this way to the V.I.P. room!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Is that a challenge for us to "bring it owl?"

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: A man who gets down right to business, eh?

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Alright! Let's go!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: V.I.P. room!? So there is some sort of connection between Crocodile and them...

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Great! This might be a chance to send all these pirates to the gallows!

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: By the way, what the heck's a VIP?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: A cunning plan.

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: Most coming, indeed.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Cunning, my ass! You guys just ran head- first into his trap!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: God! You guys are all a bunch of idiots!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Hey the hallway splits off into two directions!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Pirates

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: The V.I.P. room's to the left!

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: No, wait!

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: We're pirates,

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: So we gotta go right!

dialogue.speaker: Tashigi
dialogue.text: Wait, but- AGHHH!!!! It's a trap door!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: We're falling!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I don't know about you but it feels like all my strength is being sucked out...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Why, are you hungry or something?

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: Luffy!

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: Ughh!

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: GyaAA! Luffy!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: H-h-hey you, smoke monster! Y-y-you want s-s-some of this, huh!? Do ya!?

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: I'll have you know that I've finished off a explosive-human in the past!

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: ...Huh? I can't move...!?

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: it's like... i've fallen in the sea...

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: Yes, that's how you should feel...

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: H-Hey! What did you do to Luffy!?

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: The tip of my jutte is made with a sub- stance known as "seastone."

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: It's a mysterious stone said to be only found in a certain area of the ocean...

summary: This part develops crocodile watches the pieces move and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
